about.description: I grew up taking things apart to understand them. Now I build them from scratch. Embedded systems, robotics, AI — not as buzzwords, but as tools for making things that actually work in the real world. Still learning. Still building. Always curious.

skills.name: Embedded Programming (C, C++, Python, Verilog, VHDL)
Communication Protocols (I2C, SPI, UART, CAN, USB)
Mechanical Design (3D printing, Laser Cutting, Sheet Metal)

description: Worked directly with international clients to translate system requirements into hardware and firmware solutions.
Designed production-ready hardware including schematic capture, PCB layout, and manufacturing documentation.
Developed embedded applications for sensor systems, battery-powered devices, and robotics platforms.
Provided remote technical consultation and design recommendations.
Designed and validated multi-layer PCBs based on ESP32 MCUs, performing schematic review, signal integrity checks, and system-level debugging to ensure reliable integration.
Prepared complete manufacturing documentation (BOM, CPL, Gerber, assembly drawings, schematics).
Developed low-level firmware in C for ESP32 platforms, integrating multiple sensors and implementing efficient power management strategies.
Integrated hardware subsystems into final products, including PCB assembly, 3D-printed enclosures, switch integration, wiring harness design, and power distribution.
Conducted functional validation, electrical testing, and iterative design improvements to optimize performance, reliability, and manufacturability.
Created internal documentation and test procedures to support manufacturing and system validation.
Developed and tested Slamware-based mobile robotic systems for indoor navigation.
Utilized Slamware SDK for localization and mapping integration.
Designed mechanical structures using SolidWorks and fabricated prototypes via CNC and 3D printing.
Supported system integration and validation testing.
Contributed to development of ROS-based mobile robotic platforms.
Implemented C++/Python nodes for sensor processing and motion control.
Applied SLAM and kinematic modeling for autonomous navigation tasks.
